mission.title: spire: Node attestor azure_imds runtime error / plugin panic
mission.description: Node attestor azure_imds runtime error / plugin panic. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify spire troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your spire deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n spire --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference" external=false plugin_name=azure_imds plugin_type=NodeAttestor stack=`

steps.title: Review spire configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get configmap -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=spire
```
Hello,

We have tested attesting agent nodes with the [azure_imds](https://github.com/spiffe/spire/blob/main/doc/plugin_server_nodeattestor_azure_imds.md) node attestor.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Node attestor azure_imds runtime error / plugin panic
steps.description:
Azure IMDS node attestation for VMSS instances.

VMSS NICs don't always have a Network Security Group attached. `parseNetworkInterfaceConfig` was dereferencing `NetworkSecurityGroup.ID` unconditionally, causing a plugin panic when attesting VMSS nodes with no NSG on their NIC.

added nil checks for `Properties`, `NetworkSecurityGroup`, and `NetworkSecurityGroup.ID`. if there's no NSG the `SecurityGroup` field is left as zero-value. also guard `ipconfig.Properties` and `Subnet` against nil in

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Node attestor azure_imds runtime error / plugin… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n spire --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n spire --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference" external=false plugin_name=azure_imds plugin_type=NodeAttestor stack=` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
Azure IMDS node attestation for VMSS instances.

VMSS NICs don't always have a Network Security Group attached. `parseNetworkInterfaceConfig` was dereferencing `NetworkSecurityGroup.ID` unconditionally, causing a plugin panic when attesting VMSS nodes with no NSG on their NIC.

added nil checks for `Properties`, `NetworkSecurityGroup`, and `NetworkSecurityGroup.ID`.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
